question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 58.00% on 2026-04-11 for the Metaculus question "Will the U.S. conduct a ground invasion of Iran before May 2026?"?
summary:
This meta-prediction asks whether the Metaculus community prediction on "Will the U.S. conduct a ground invasion of Iran before May 2026?" will exceed 58% on April 11, 2026. The CP is currently at exactly 58% (as of March 29), having jumped 16 points in one week from the Pentagon ground operations planning news.

There is an active US-Israel war with Iran (since Feb 28). The Pentagon is preparing for limited ground operations (Kharg Island seizure, coastal raids), with 82nd Airborne and two Marine Expeditionary Units deploying. However, Trump hasn't authorized ground entry, and Secretary Rubio said objectives can be achieved without ground troops.

Three scenarios drive the outcome: (1) Ground troops enter Iran (~25% chance) → CP surges well above 58% or question resolves YES, making this meta-question resolve YES. (2) Continued buildup without entry (~67%) → CP fluctuates around 58% with slight upward bias from ongoing escalation. (3) Ceasefire (~8%) → CP drops well below 58%.

The asymmetric upside from the ground entry scenario is the key driver. In the most likely scenario (continued buildup), CP is roughly a coin flip to stay above 58%. Combined: ~59% probability.

factors.description: Active war with Pentagon preparing ground operations (WaPo March 28). 82nd Airborne, 2 MEUs deploying. Strongest driver of CP upward movement.
CP momentum: jumped 16 points in one week (42%→58%), driven by escalation news. Recency-weighted median amplifies recent upward forecasts.
If ground troops enter Iran (~25% chance in 13-day window), CP surges well above 58% or target question resolves YES directly. Asymmetric upside scenario.
Trump hasn't authorized ground operations; Rubio said 'can achieve objectives without ground troops'. Creates ceiling on CP without actual entry.
CP exactly at threshold (58%). No buffer - even modest downward movement resolves NO. In buildup-but-no-entry scenario, CP is roughly 50/50 above or below 58%.
April 6 energy strikes deadline could be escalation catalyst if Iran doesn't comply with Strait of Hormuz demands.
Peace talks stalled but not dead. Small chance (~8%) of ceasefire/de-escalation that would crash CP.
MAGA backlash and Trump's 'no forever wars' promise act as political constraints against ground invasion authorization.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.59 (59% probability YES)**

The CP on the Iran ground invasion question is currently at exactly the 58% threshold. The key dynamics:

- **Active war + ground preparations** provide strong upward pressure on CP. Pentagon is planning "weeks of ground operations" with major unit deployments underway.
- **~25% chance of actual ground entry** in the next 13 days, which would push CP well above 58% or resolve the target question YES directly. This asymmetric upside is the main reason the probability is above 50%.
- **In the most likely scenario** (continued buildup without entry, ~67%), the CP is roughly a coin flip to be above or below 58%, with slight upward lean from ongoing escalation.
- **Small ceasefire risk** (~8%) would crash the CP below 58%.
- The combined probability from scenario decomposition yields approximately **59%**.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42542) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-04-11 01:50:34 is higher than 58.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 58.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-04-11 01:50:34, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
